title: pinus greggii subsp. australis
intro: Pinus greggii subsp. australis, also known as the Sonoran pinyon pine, is a subspecies of pine tree native to the southwestern region of North America. The medicinal properties of Pinus greggii subsp. australis include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its resin and essential oil content. The health benefits of consuming this plant's seeds and nuts include their high levels of antioxidants and minerals such as magnesium, phosphorus, and potassium, which may help lower cholesterol levels and improve overall cardiovascular health. Pinus greggii subsp. australis is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, and is also used as a food source, with its seeds and nuts being edible and rich in nutrients.
